title: What to Know About Iran’s Kharg Island
summary: The Persian Gulf island, targeted by U.S. strikes, is home to Iran’s main oil export terminal. Any disruption could jolt global energy markets.

title: Trump’s Move to Seize Oil Tankers Costs the U.S. Tens of Millions of Dollars
summary: Although President Trump said seizing tankers would be a financial boon, the cost of maintaining just one aging ship has already reached $47 million.

title: More Marines and Warships Being Sent to Middle East, U.S. Officials Say
summary: Iran’s response to days of aerial bombardment and long-range artillery strikes has proved more resilient than Trump administration officials anticipated.

title: Judge Quashes Justice Dept.’s Subpoenas of Fed, Crippling Its Pursuit of Trump’s Rivals
summary: Judge James E. Boasberg derided the U.S. attorney’s office in Washington for pursuing a case against Jerome H. Powell that appeared to be motivated by President Trump’s desire for vengeance.

title: Kennedy Center’s President, Richard Grenell, Is Leaving After a Tumultuous Year
summary: Since Richard Grenell was appointed by President Trump, the arts center has endured waves of cancellations and departures. It will soon close for lengthy renovations.

title: Judge Temporarily Blocks Trump Demand for Student Race Data
summary: A group of Democratic attorneys general had sued to overturn the Trump administration’s new policy that demanded the past seven years of student application data.

title: Temple Israel Staff Raced to Save Lives in Michigan Synagogue Attack
summary: “I didn’t breathe until I knew all of the kids were OK,” one teacher recalled after a truck crashed through the doors of a Michigan synagogue this week.

title: Family Members of Michigan Synagogue Attacker Died in Airstrike in Lebanon
summary: The man, a U.S. citizen born in Lebanon, lost family members during an airstrike there last week. The attack on a Michigan synagogue rattled Jewish communities across America.

title: The Michigan Synagogue Attacker Was a Quiet Restaurant Worker
summary: Days before the antisemitic violence, an imam recalled seeing Ayman Mohamad Ghazali at a service for his relatives who had been killed in the war in Lebanon.

title: The Fall of Noma’s René Redzepi Reverberates in the Restaurant World
summary: In the industry where René Redzepi reigned, fellow chefs are debating how, and how much, restaurant kitchens can change.

title: TikTok Investors Set to Pay $10 Billion Fee to Trump Administration
summary: The large fee is the latest example of the White House’s inserting itself into corporate deal making in unusual and aggressive ways.

title: The Complicated Oscars Night Feelings Over ‘One Battle After Another’ | Cannonball with Wesley Morris
summary: It’s a movie about Black feminist revolutionaries that some Black feminists are calling a 911 emergency.

title: His Film Is Spain’s Submission to the Oscars. He’s Not Sure How Spanish It Is.
summary: Spanish cinema has entered a new and more diverse era, film experts say. Oliver Laxe, the director of Oscar-nominated “Sirat,” embodies the shift.

title: After 17 Oscar Nominations, Diane Warren Is Still Chasing Her First Win
summary: For Diane Warren, the work is the reward. But after 16 losses, an original song Oscar would be nice, too.

title: Before Deadly Old Dominion Shooting, Parallel Lives of 2 Soldiers Diverged
summary: Lt. Col. Brandon Shah flew hundreds of combat missions as a war-zone helicopter pilot before being shot and killed in his Old Dominion University classroom by a radicalized military veteran.

title: Gunman in Old Dominion Shooting Appeared to Target ROTC, Court Records Show
summary: Before opening fire in a university classroom, the gunman asked people if they were there for a Reserve Officers Training Corps event, court documents said.

title: Sam Page Poised to Beat Phil Berger in High Stakes N.C. Primary
summary: A formal tally on Friday showed Sam Page ahead of Phil Berger, the powerful longtime leader of the State Senate. A recount is expected but experts say that is unlikely to flip the results.

title: With Endorsement, Trump Clears Oklahoma Senate Path
summary: The move, as other Republicans confirmed they would not run, all but assured Representative Kevin Hern would succeed Senator Markwayne Mullin.

title: What to Know About AI Political Campaign Ads During Election Season
summary: Wait, that’s not the real James Talarico …

title: New York Academy of Art Gives Away Money Donated by Jeffrey Epstein
summary: The school also said that it would review policies about philanthropy and donor engagement after new revelations about the disgraced financier were made public.

title: At Dinners, Over Jokes With Comedians, Epstein Honed His Networking
summary: In the years after his 2008 conviction for soliciting prostitution, Jeffrey Epstein rebuilt his reputation by hosting gatherings with leaders in all sorts of trades, including comedy.

title: Why I’m Suing Grammarly
summary: A tech company made a deepfake of my mind. I’m fighting back.

title: Any Way You Look at It, Netanyahu Wins
summary: The war in Iran is a victory for the Israeli prime minister.

title: Chemical Smell at Control Center Halts Traffic at Washington and Baltimore Airports
summary: The ground delays, which also affected the airport serving Richmond, Va., were expected to last until at least midnight, according to the F.A.A., which said the smell had affected air traffic controllers.

title: Protesters Accused of Antifa Ties Found Guilty of Support for Terrorism
summary: The guilty verdicts marked the first time that terrorism charges had been successfully brought against purported members of antifa.

title: Charges Dropped Against Teenagers Whose Teacher Died in Prank Gone Wrong
summary: The teacher’s relatives said they supported “getting the charges dropped for all involved” after a student prank led to his death last week in Georgia.

title: John F. Burns, Prize-winning Foreign Correspondent for The Times, Dies at 81
summary: In a 40-year career that brought him two Pulitzers, he reported from trouble spots around the world, eloquently conveying the chaos of war.
